title: Hedyotis yoderi
intro: Hedyotis yoderi is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "Bitterflower" due to its bitter taste. Traditionally, Hedyotis yoderi has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis yoderi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Hedyotis yoderi is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, with the decoction being one of the most common preparations.
taxonomy: Hedyotis yoderi is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the madder family and comprises approximately 600 genera. The plant is native to the southeastern United States and is typically found in areas with well-drained acidic soils. It is classified as a member of the Rubiaceae family within the order Gentianales. As a member of the Gentianales order, Hedyotis yoderi is also closely related to other plants in this order, such as gentians and foxgloves.
